Package com.dtwthalion.ttio.protection

Class EncryptionManager

java.lang.Object
com.dtwthalion.ttio.protection.EncryptionManager
public final class EncryptionManager extends Object
AES-256-GCM encryption/decryption for TTI-O datasets.

Key parameters: 32-byte key, 12-byte random IV, 128-bit (16-byte) authentication tag.

API status: Stable.

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIOEncryptionManager, Python ttio.encryption.

Since:
0.6

  • Field Details

    • WK_ALG_ML_KEM_1024

      public static final int WK_ALG_ML_KEM_1024
      v0.8 M49: ML-KEM-1024 envelope. Writes set the opt_pqc_preview feature flag on the enclosing file.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • encrypt

      public static EncryptionManager.EncryptResult encrypt(byte[] plaintext, byte[] key)
      Encrypt plaintext bytes with AES-256-GCM (default cipher suite). Shorthand for encrypt(byte[], byte[], String) with algorithm="aes-256-gcm".
      Parameters:
      plaintext - data to encrypt
      key - 32-byte AES-256 key
      Returns:
      EncryptResult containing ciphertext, iv, and tag

    • encrypt

      public static EncryptionManager.EncryptResult encrypt(byte[] plaintext, byte[] key, String algorithm)
      Encrypt plaintext bytes with the named cipher suite. v0.7 M48: algorithm selection is routed through CipherSuite. Only "aes-256-gcm" is active in v0.7; reserved suites raise CipherSuite.UnsupportedAlgorithmException.
      Since:
      0.7

    • decrypt

      public static byte[] decrypt(byte[] ciphertext, byte[] iv, byte[] tag, byte[] key)
      Decrypt ciphertext given iv and tag (AES-256-GCM default). Shorthand for decrypt(byte[], byte[], byte[], byte[], String).
      Parameters:
      ciphertext - encrypted bytes (without tag)
      iv - 12-byte initialisation vector
      tag - 16-byte authentication tag
      key - 32-byte AES-256 key
      Returns:
      plaintext bytes

    • decrypt

      public static byte[] decrypt(byte[] ciphertext, byte[] iv, byte[] tag, byte[] key, String algorithm)
      Decrypt with the named cipher suite. v0.7 M48: algorithm selection via CipherSuite.
      Since:
      0.7

    • encryptChannel

      public static EncryptionManager.EncryptResult encryptChannel(double[] data, byte[] key)
      Encrypt a double[] signal channel. Serialises to little-endian bytes, then encrypts.
      Parameters:
      data - signal samples
      key - 32-byte AES-256 key
      Returns:
      EncryptResult

    • decryptChannel

      public static double[] decryptChannel(byte[] ciphertext, byte[] iv, byte[] tag, byte[] key, int originalCount)
      Decrypt a signal channel back to double[].
      Parameters:
      ciphertext - encrypted bytes
      iv - 12-byte IV
      tag - 16-byte tag
      key - 32-byte key
      originalCount - number of doubles in the original channel
      Returns:
      decrypted signal samples

    • wrapKey

      public static byte[] wrapKey(byte[] dek, byte[] kek)
      Wrap a data-encryption key (DEK) with a key-encryption key (KEK). Emits the v1.2 versioned AES-256-GCM blob (71 bytes).
      Parameters:
      dek - 32-byte data-encryption key
      kek - 32-byte key-encryption key
      Returns:
      v1.2 wrapped blob (71 bytes for AES-256-GCM).

    • wrapKey

      public static byte[] wrapKey(byte[] dek, byte[] kek, boolean legacyV1)
      Wrap a DEK with an explicit version selector.
      Parameters:
      legacyV1 - when true, emits the 60-byte v1.1 layout for regression fixtures; callers targeting production should use wrapKey(byte[], byte[]).
      Since:
      0.7

    • wrapKey

      public static byte[] wrapKey(byte[] dek, byte[] kek, boolean legacyV1, String algorithm)
      Wrap a DEK with an explicit version selector and cipher suite. v0.8 M49: adds algorithm="ml-kem-1024" for post-quantum key encapsulation. kek is interpreted per algorithm — 32-byte symmetric for AES-GCM, 1568-byte ML-KEM public key for ML-KEM.
      Since:
      0.7

    • unwrapKey

      public static byte[] unwrapKey(byte[] wrappedBlob, byte[] kek, String algorithm)
      Unwrap a DEK from a wrapped-key blob with an explicit cipher suite selector. Distinct from the blob-length-dispatched unwrapKey(byte[], byte[]) to support ML-KEM-1024 (where the reader must already know it's holding the decapsulation private key, not a symmetric AES KEK).
      Since:
      0.8

    • packBlobV2

      public static byte[] packBlobV2(int algorithmId, byte[] ciphertext, byte[] metadata)
      Pack a v1.2 wrapped-key blob. Public for cross-language interop tests (M51) and for algorithm-specific wrappers introduced in M48.
      Since:
      0.7

    • unpackBlobV2

      public static EncryptionManager.WrappedBlobV2 unpackBlobV2(byte[] blob)
      Unpack a v1.2 wrapped-key blob.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the magic or layout is malformed.
      Since:
      0.7

    • unwrapKey

      public static byte[] unwrapKey(byte[] wrappedBlob, byte[] kek)
      Unwrap a DEK from a wrapped-key blob. Dispatches on blob length: exactly 60 bytes ⇒ v1.1 legacy; anything else ⇒ v1.2 versioned.
      Parameters:
      wrappedBlob - the blob produced by wrapKey(byte[], byte[]).
      kek - 32-byte key-encryption key.
      Returns:
      32-byte DEK.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the blob uses a reserved algorithm id (e.g. ML-KEM-1024 without the pqc_preview build profile enabled in M49).

    • testKey

      public static byte[] testKey()
      Canonical test key: key[i] = (byte)((0xA5 ^ (i * 3)) & 0xFF) for i in [0,32).
      Returns:
      32-byte deterministic test key

    • encryptIntensityChannelInRun

      public static void encryptIntensityChannelInRun(String filePath, String runName, byte[] key)
      Encrypt the intensity_values dataset of the named MS run inside the .tio file at filePath, in place.

      Locates /study/ms_runs/<runName>/signal_channels/, reads the plaintext intensity_values dataset, encrypts its bytes with AES-256-GCM, writes intensity_values_encrypted (byte array packed as int32 padded to 4-byte boundary), plus sibling intensity_iv and intensity_tag datasets, plus attributes intensity_ciphertext_bytes (int64), intensity_original_count (int64), and intensity_algorithm ("AES-256-GCM"), then deletes the original intensity_values dataset.

      Idempotent: if the channel is already encrypted, returns silently without re-encrypting.

      Parameters:
      filePath - absolute path to the .tio file
      runName - run key under /study/ms_runs/
      key - 32-byte AES-256 key

    • decryptIntensityChannelInRun

      public static byte[] decryptIntensityChannelInRun(String filePath, String runName, byte[] key)
      Decrypt the previously-encrypted intensity channel for the named run. Returns plaintext bytes (length = original_count * 8 for float64). The on-disk file is NOT modified.
      Parameters:
      filePath - absolute path to the .tio file
      runName - run key
      key - 32-byte AES-256 key
      Returns:
      plaintext bytes

    • decryptIntensityChannelInRunInPlace

      public static void decryptIntensityChannelInRunInPlace(String filePath, String runName, byte[] key)
      v1.1.1: persist-to-disk decrypt counterpart to encryptIntensityChannelInRun(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, byte[]). Opens the .tio file read-write, decrypts the named run's intensity_values_encrypted dataset, writes plaintext back as a new intensity_values float64 dataset, deletes the encrypted siblings (intensity_values_encrypted, intensity_iv, intensity_tag), and removes the channel-level attributes intensity_ciphertext_bytes, intensity_original_count, and intensity_algorithm. The root @encrypted attribute is left in place — callers that want a fully unprotected file should use SpectralDataset.decryptInPlace(String, byte[]).

      Idempotent: returns silently if the run is already plaintext.

      Parameters:
      filePath - absolute path to the .tio file
      runName - run key under /study/ms_runs/
      key - 32-byte AES-256 key
